ogTitle: Cape Le Grand vs Cape Range National Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cape Le Grand and Cape Range National Park.
heading: Cape Le Grand vs Cape Range National Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cape Le Grand, Western Australia and Cape Range National Park, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cape Range National Park has a lower overall climate risk score (26/100) compared to Cape Le Grand (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cape Le Grand or Cape Range National Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cape Le Grand has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Cape Range National Park scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cape Le Grand scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Cape Range National Park scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cape Le Grand or Cape Range National Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cape Range National Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
